steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content: The word “However” introduces a direct contradiction: the passage states that horizontal gene transfer “has rarely been observed among eukaryotes,” while the underlined sentence reveals that “new studies suggest...it is more common in eukaryotes than originally thought.” This reversal signals that the earlier understanding was incomplete or inaccurate.

steps.title: Apply the signal to find what the sentence does
steps.content: By introducing new studies showing HGT is “more common in eukaryotes than originally thought,” the underlined sentence challenges the earlier claim that HGT is rare in eukaryotes — signaling that this conventional belief may be inaccurate. This is exactly what choice A describes. Choices B, C, and D either describe earlier parts of the passage, make unsupported claims, or reference a distinction established earlier. That matches choice A — A is correct.
